title: podman cp does not rename symlink target when copying from container to host
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Using the `podman cp` command, I am able to copy the target of a symlink to a container as described in the [docs](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-cp.1.html):

> If src_path is local and is a symbolic link, the symbolic target, is copied by default.

In this case, the symlink target is copied to the container but is renamed to the symlink name. If I copy a symlink from the container back to the host, it will actually copy the target without renaming it.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Spawn a new container, e.g. `podman run -d --rm --name test alpine`
2. Try to copy symlinks from and to the container and notice the different behavior (see below)

**Describe the results you received:**

1. Inside the container, create a regular file and a symlink to it
    ```bash
    $ cd /tmp
    $ touch 1
    $ ln -s 1 2
    ```
2. On the host, try to copy the symlink to the host. Podman will copy the regular file without renaming.
    ```bash
    $ podman cp test:/tmp/2 .
    $ ls
    1
    ```
3. On the host, create a new symlink to the file and copy it to the container
    ```bash
    $ ln -s 1 3
    $ podman cp 3 test:/tmp/
    ```
4. Inside the container, notice that 1 has been copied and renamed to 3
    ```bash
    /tmp $ ls -la
    total 4
    drwxrwxrwt    1 root     root             6 Oct 14 15:24 .
    dr-xr-xr-x    1 root     root            34 Oct 14 15:17 ..
    -rw-r--r--    1 root     root             0 Oct 14 15:19 1
    lrwxrwxrwx    1 root     root             1 Oct 14 15:20 2 -> 1
    -rw-r--r--    1 root     root             0 Oct 14 15:19 3
    ```
5. On the host, copy back the whole folder and realize that now symlinks are resolved and included
    ```bash
    $ podman cp test:/tmp .
    $ ls -ls tmp/
    total 4
    drwxrwxrwx. 1 philipp philipp  6 Oct 14 17:24 .
    drwx------. 1 philipp philipp 10 Oct 14 17:27 ..
    -rw-r--r--. 1 philipp philipp  0 Oct 14 17:19 1
    lrwxrwxrwx. 1 philipp philipp  1 Oct 14 17:20 2 -> 1
    -rw-r--r--. 1 philipp philipp  0 Oct 14 17:19 3
    ```
**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected a more consistent way of handling symlinks, either by always resolving them to their target and properly renaming them or by creating proper replacement symlinks.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Thu Aug 11 16:42:17 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: I have been working on podman cp --link issue, when I have time, but have not progressed as much as I would have liked, might also fix this problem.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Currently running podman version 4.5.1, behavior is still identical to what I described above. Not an issue for me personally since the project I was working on changed the CI in order to work around this issue, but still I think that the current behavior is rather inconsistent and counter intuitive for the users
